_note: Central-bank policy rates are maintained MANUALLY (no reliable free API). Update after CB meetings and bump as_of. The dashboard displays as_of so staleness is visible.
_outlook_note: outlook = Claude's discretionary 6-month view (rate forecast + long/short call on the currency vs USD), written on outlook_as_of. Opinion, not advice. Refresh by asking Claude to re-assess.

outlook.rationale: Cutting cycle likely continues: cooling labor market and moderating inflation point to 1–2 more cuts; mild headwind for the dollar overall.
ECB at/near terminal while the Fed keeps cutting — the rate gap narrows in EUR's favor; current downtrend is the main counterargument.
Gradual BoE cuts erode GBP's carry edge; sticky services inflation vs weak growth keeps it two-sided.
Only major bank hiking while the Fed cuts — strongest rate-convergence story on the board; has been early/wrong before, and spot trend is still against it.
SNB pinned at zero and resisting negative rates; CHF is a risk-off hedge more than a carry story.
BoC near the end of its cycle; CAD tracks oil and US demand more than rates from here.
Best carry among majors after the Fed's cuts, RBA patient, and the pair already trades above its 200-day SMA — carry and trend align.
Deep cutting cycle mostly done but carry is gone and the trend is weak; needs a China/commodity impulse to matter.
Easing continues as disinflation progresses; spot TRY almost certainly weaker vs USD in 6 months (managed slide) — note the huge carry can still out-earn the depreciation, which is a separate trade.
